query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Unbecom', 'Not flattering, attractive or appropriate', 'What seemed unbecom at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unbelief', 'A lack (or rejection) of belief', 'Scholars noted a striking unbelief between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unbridl', 'Not controlled or restrained', 'The reviewer described the tone as unbridl, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uncanny', 'Strange or mysterious, especially in an unsettling way', 'The reviewer described the tone as uncanny, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unconscionable', 'Unscrupulous, not guided by conscience', 'The passage presents a unconscionable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unconscious', 'Not awake', 'What seemed unconscious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unconventional', 'Acting outside of the norm', 'Scholars noted a striking unconventional between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unction', 'Anointing, as a medical treatment or religious rite', 'The essay highlights the unction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unctuous', 'Excessively flattering or ingratiating', 'What seemed unctuous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undeceive', 'To free from misconception, deception or error', 'Over time, the community learned to undeceive its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undercharge', 'To charge less than the correct amount', 'The author hoped the new policy would undercharge tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underexpose', 'Expose (film or an image) for too short a time', 'Scholars noted a striking underexpose between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underhand', 'Sneaky', 'Scholars noted a striking underhand between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underl', 'A subordinate', 'Scholars noted a striking underl between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underlie', 'To lie in a position directly beneath', 'Critics argued that the plan might underlie the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underman', 'Insufficiently furnished with men; short-handed', 'The essay highlights the underman of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undermine', 'To weaken or damage', '“His behavior undermines his credibility.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underpin', 'To support or reinforce', '“The theory is underpinned by scientific evidence.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underpinn', 'A system of supports beneath a wall', 'Scholars noted a striking underpinn between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underrate', 'To underestimate, to make too low a rate or estimate', 'Over time, the community learned to underrate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underscore', 'To underline, to mark a line beneath text', 'Critics argued that the plan might underscore the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undersell', 'To sell goods for a lower price than a competitor', 'Critics argued that the plan might undersell the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Understate', 'To minimise or downplay', 'The author hoped the new policy would understate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Underwrite', 'To write under, especially under other written matter', 'Over time, the community learned to underwrite its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undulat', 'Rising and falling like waves', 'Her undulat became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undulate', 'Move in a wave-like pattern', 'Over time, the community learned to undulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undulation', 'A wavelike motion', 'Her undulation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Undulous', 'Undulating', 'The reviewer described the tone as undulous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unduly', 'Excessively', 'The results were unduly linked to changes in public policy.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unequivocal', 'Clear and unambiguous', '“Her statement was unequivocal and firm.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unfavorable', 'Disadvantageous, adverse, unsuitable', 'The passage presents a unfavorable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unfeign', 'Sincere', 'The essay highlights the unfeign of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unfetter', 'Free from restraint', 'Her unfetter became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unflappable', 'Remaining composed and level-headed at all times', 'The reviewer described the tone as unflappable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unflinch', 'Staying committed despite any difficulty, steadfast', 'The essay highlights the unflinch of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unfound', 'Not based on facts', '“The rumor was completely unfounded.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unfrock', 'To remove from status as a member of a clergy', 'The author hoped the new policy would unfrock tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ungainly', 'Clumsy, lacking grace', 'She spoke ungainly, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unguent', 'Therapeutic skin cream with medicinal ingredients', 'What seemed unguent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unicellular', 'Describing any microorganism that has a single cell', 'The essay highlights the unicellular of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unification', 'The process of unifying or uniting', 'The essay highlights the unification of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uniform', 'Identical or consistent', 'Her uniform became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unify', 'To become one, merge, combine', 'Critics argued that the plan might unify the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unilateral', 'Done by one side only', 'The essay highlights the unilateral of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unimpeachable', 'Above suspicion, impossible to discredit', 'The author hoped the new policy would unimpeachable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Uninhibit', 'Not restrained by social convention or usage', 'The passage presents a uninhibit portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unintelligible', 'Not capable of being understood', 'What seemed unintelligible at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unisonant', 'Of a single sound, producing the same sound', 'Scholars noted a striking unisonant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Unitarian', 'A supporter of unity or centralization', 'Scholars noted a striking unitarian between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Univalence', '(chemistry) the condition of having a valence of one', 'Scholars noted a striking univalence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
